# Motivation

## Why Compliant Robots Matter

- Physical robots often operate near people, tools, fixtures, and uncertain environments.
- Contact is not a failure mode; it is part of the task.
- Compliance helps robots absorb uncertainty while maintaining purposeful behavior.

TODO: Insert image of a compliant robot manipulating an object at `assets/images/compliant_robot_contact.png`.

---

## Contact-Rich Manipulation Is Difficult

- Small pose errors can create large force errors.
- Friction, deformation, and contact switches are hard to model.
- Purely geometric plans often fail when the environment pushes back.

TODO: Insert contact-rich manipulation failure/success figure at `figures/contact_rich_challenges.png`.

---

## Why Learning from Demonstrations Helps

- Demonstrations encode task timing, recovery behavior, and implicit contact strategies.
- Expert data can be easier to collect than dense reward functions.
- Learning can capture behaviors that are difficult to specify analytically.

$$
\mathcal{D} = \{(o_t, a_t)\}_{t=1}^{N}
$$

---

## From Demonstrations to Control

- The learning problem: infer a policy from demonstrations.
- The control problem: execute that policy safely on a physical robot.
- The central tension: expressive learned behavior must respect real-time and safety constraints.
